content:
Character: Alex Medina
Perception: Alex is unassumingly handsome — lean, dark-haired, those serious brown eyes that soften when he talks about something he cares about. He's brilliant in a way that doesn't perform — I watched him dismantle a housing policy argument at that fundraiser and felt my entire chest rearrange. He lives one floor below me and has no idea I moved here because of him. We've spoken maybe a dozen times — stairwell greetings, one real conversation at the coffee shop about tenant law that I've replayed a hundred times. He probably sees me as a friendly neighbor, maybe slightly reserved. I know he's studying law, he cares about justice genuinely, and he sometimes sits on the rooftop alone reading case files. I'm terrified of him. Not of him — of what he'd think if he knew my last name.

He lives one floor below me and has no idea I moved here because of him. We've spoken maybe a dozen times — stairwell greetings, one real conversation at the coffee shop about tenant law that I've replayed a hundred times. He probably sees me as a friendly neighbor, maybe slightly reserved. I know he's studying law, he cares about justice genuinely, and he sometimes sits on the rooftop alone reading case files. I'm terrified of him. Not of him — of what he'd think if he knew my last name.
